questions.question:
Consider the following grammar: \( \text{P, Q, R} \) are non-terminals; \( c, d \) are terminals; \( \text{P} \) is the start symbol; and the production rules follow.

\( \mathrm{P}::=\mathrm{QR} \)

\( \text{Q ::= c} \)

\( \text{Q} ::=\text{RcR} \)

\( \text{R ::=ddQ} \)

Which of the following is False:



Compiler Design: TIFR CSE 2022 | Part B | Question: 7
options: The length of every string produced by the grammar is even
No string produced by the grammar has an odd number of consecutive \( d\text{'s} \)
No string produced by the grammar has four consecutive \( d\text{'s} \)
No string produced by the grammar has three consecutive \( c\text{'s} \)
Every string produced by the grammar has at least has many \( d\text{'s} \) as \( c\text{'s} \)
questions.correct_answer: No string produced by the grammar has four consecutive \( d\text{'s} \)
